AWD · Gotchas

Handling User Input and Data Conversion

← Back to Course Package
⚠️ Missing Node.js Type Definitions
TypeScript does not automatically recognize native Node.js modules like 'process' or 'readline' without the @types/node package. If you miss this, your code will fail to compile with 'Cannot find name' errors despite the code being logically correct.
⚠️ The Hanging Terminal Trap
Failing to explicitly call rl.close() after processing user input keeps the Node.js event loop active. This causes your application to hang indefinitely in the terminal, forcing a manual crash (Ctrl+C) instead of a clean exit.
⚠️ Partial Parsing Pitfalls
Functions like parseFloat() will successfully extract '55' from '55abc' instead of failing, which can lead to silent data corruption. If you don't validate the entire string, your app will process 'dirty' data that should have been rejected as invalid input.
⚠️ Async Context Requirements
The 'await' keyword for rl.question only functions inside an async function or a file explicitly marked as an ES Module in package.json. Without this configuration, your code will throw a syntax error, preventing you from using the modern, readable way of handling input.
⚠️ The 'Any' Type Escape Hatch
Using the 'any' type tells TypeScript to stop checking your code for errors on that specific variable. This effectively turns off the compiler's safety features, allowing bugs to reach production that TypeScript was specifically designed to catch.
